title: Dryopteris hallieri
taxonomy: Dryopteris hallieri is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which includes over 250 species of ferns. This species is native to South America and is characterized by its distinctive frond shape and growth habit. Dryopteris hallieri is classified as a terrestrial fern, meaning it grows in soil rather than on other plants. The classification of Dryopteris hallieri is further supported by its unique combination of morphological and anatomical features.
